pi_items.source: Explicitly checked in export_result.sh and set in setup_task.sh.
Explicitly checked in export_result.sh and expected by verifier.py logic.
Enforced by prompt instructions and verifier checks (forecast_rows between 34 and 38).

Python/R empirical analysis of tsibble::PBS dataset for ATC2 'L01'. The historical monthly sum ranges up to ~49k. A standard Holt-Winters/ARIMA forecast predicts a mean around ~48k, completely contradicting the 100k+ verifier threshold.
Python/R empirical analysis of tsibble::PBS dataset for ATC2 'L01'. Historical maximum is 49,213, meaning a 3-year prediction maximum would be far lower than 400k.
Prompt instructions and setup_task.sh strictly request ATC2 target 'L01'.

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on real historical Australian Pharmaceutical Benefits Scheme (PBS) data from the 'tsibble' R package. While output directory requirements, target files, and a 36-month forecast horizon are accurately verified against task scripts, a critical programmatic flaw was discovered in the validation logic. The verifier incorrectly assumes L01 scripts average ~200,000 monthly and strictly requires the forecasted mean to exceed 100,000 to award points. In reality, empirical aggregation of L01 scripts across all concession and type subgroups yields a historical monthly average of just ~23,080 and a peak of ~49,213. Therefore, an agent producing a factually correct forecast (around 48,000 scripts) will falsely fail the verifier's strict magnitude check, rendering the task effectively unscorable unless data is manipulated.
